Description

Offered for sale is an authentic Omega Seamaster Diver 300M reference 2561.80, the classic “Bond-era” midsize model featuring the iconic blue wave dial.

This late-1990s example carries the sought-after electric blue dial with wave pattern and matching blue unidirectional bezel. The 36mm case size wears extremely well and offers a clean, balanced look compared to modern oversized divers. Demand for these midsize quartz models has been steadily increasing due to comfort and versatility.

Bracelet will fit approximately a 6.7” wrist.

⸻

Specifications
• 36mm stainless steel case
• Blue wave dial
• Blue unidirectional rotating bezel
• Omega quartz movement
• 300m / 1000ft water resistance
• Sapphire crystal
• Original stainless steel bracelet

⸻

James Bond Era Classic

Offered for sale is the iconic Omega Seamaster Professional 300M midsize, reference 2562.80.00.

This is the classic wave dial Seamaster made famous during the James Bond era. Stainless steel case, blue wave dial, skeleton hands, and the unmistakable dive bezel design that defined late 90s and early 2000s Omega.

The midsize case wears incredibly well. Comfortable, balanced, and extremely versatile for daily wear.

Quartz movement for reliability and grab and go convenience. 300 meters of water resistance. True professional dive watch heritage.

This example is fitted on a black rubber strap for a modern sport look. The dial and hands present beautifully with strong character.

The Omega Seamaster Diver 300M Chronograph Rose Gold & Ceramic Black Dial 44mm Ref. 210.62.44.51.01.001 is a bold and functional luxury dive watch designed for precision and performance. This 44mm model is crafted from 18K Sedna gold and features a black ceramic bezel ring with a laser-ablated diving scale for enhanced durability and clarity. The polished black ceramic dial is laser-engraved with the Seamaster’s signature wave pattern in positive relief and includes a date window. The subdial rings and hands are made from 18K Sedna gold, while the central hands and indexes, also in 18K Sedna gold, are filled with white Super-LumiNova for optimal legibility. Driven by the OMEGA Master Chronometer Calibre 9901, this self-winding chronograph offers a 60-hour power reserve and unparalleled precision, visible through the sapphire-crystal wave-edged caseback. Water-resistant to 300m, the watch is completed with a black rubber strap and an 18K Sedna gold pin buckle.

Payment can be accepted through wire transfer or Chrono24 Trusted Checkout. A photo/screenshot of the wire confirmation is a must it will help us to verify the payment from the bank.

All purchases, regardless of the payment method or destination of the shipped package, are subject to state and local taxes.

POLICIES:
We only carry authentic, genuine fine watches and make sure to inspect all of them for authenticity. No matter the age of the watch, we make sure to provide you with a warranty for peace of mind. All of our pictures are pictures of the actual watch being sold.

FOR BUYERS:
You will be getting EXACTLY what is visible in the pictures - NOTHING MORE. So if you don't see a receipt from the first sale, nor a price tag, nor a paper or a plastic bag from the very first sale - we simply don't have it and you cannot expect that in the parcel from us. It also happens quite often that a manual booklet is missing, even papers, or some external box - what we have on offer for you - we always show in the pictures here. If you are unsure please ask us.

OUR BUSINESS
We have a beautiful collection of Exclusive and Vintage watches. The pictures are from the actual advertised watch.

SHIPPING:
We will ship your order within 1-2 working days from the time of order. Orders will be shipped via FedEx. International Customers are responsible for the cost of the shipping along with the customs taxes and/or duties of the receiving country.

Description:
The Omega Speedmaster Silver Snoopy is a stunning 50th Anniversary tribute to the Apollo 13 mission in 1970. As one of the most desirable Omegas to date, the Snoopy features a stainless steel case with a ceramic bezel insert and a white dial with contrasting blue indices and matching subdials. Both the 9 o'clock subdial and sapphire caseback feature images of Snoopy as a tribute to the Silver Snoopy Award. This model comes on Omega's high quality fabric strap, making it durable and comfortable. Powering the Snoopy is Omega's in-house Caliber 3861 mechanical movement with 50 hours of power reserve.

The OMEGA Speedmaster is one of OMEGA’s most iconic timepieces. The Moonwatch "First Omega in Space" was inspired by the watch worn by astronaut Wally Shirra in 1962 as he orbited the earth during the "Sigma 7" mission.

This numbered edition model features a bold varnished black dial with a small seconds dial, a 30-minute recorder and 12-hour recorder, along with a central chronograph hand and a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal. The black bezel, with its tachymetric scale, is mounted on a 39.7 mm stainless steel case with symmetric lugs, and the watch is presented on a brown leather strap.

At the heart of this chronograph is OMEGA's calibre 1861, the famous manual-winding movement that was worn on the moon, which is covered by a caseback embossed with the original 1962 Seahorse.

The Omega Speedmaster Professional Apollo 11 Edition pays tribute to the first lunar landing in 1969. It preserves the heritage of the original Moonwatch while offering modern durability and precision. The stainless-steel 42 mm case features brushed and polished surfaces, a black aluminum tachymeter bezel, and a domed Hesalite crystal for an authentic look identical to the watches worn by Apollo astronauts.

The black step dial provides sharp legibility with luminous hour markers and white chronograph registers. The manual-wind Caliber 1861 movement—directly descended from the Moon-landed Caliber 321—is visible through the solid engraved caseback, inscribed “Flight-Qualified by NASA for All Manned Space Missions.”

A stainless-steel bracelet with secure folding clasp ensures balanced wear and long-term comfort. The watch includes chronograph functionality for elapsed-time measurement, tachymeter scale, and 48-hour power reserve.

This reference was produced between 2014 and 2021, marking the final generation of the pre-Master Chronometer Moonwatch before Omega introduced the Co-Axial Caliber 3861. The Apollo 11 Special Edition remains one of the most collectible and historically faithful Speedmasters, offering exceptional reliability, serviceability, and long-term value.

1962 Omega Seamaster 30. Steel case, small seconds dial, cal. 269. Recently serviced and running accurately. Brown leather strap.

Quick note on the name! the "30" isn't water resistance, it's the movement diameter. Omega sized the cal. 269 to exactly 30mm to compete in the observatory precision class that topped out at that width. It's the last version of that movement family, 17 jewels, manual wind, 18,000 bph, 42-hour reserve. It also runs a shock protection, which Omega only ever put in two calibers - this one and the 286.

The British Army issued watches with this movement. The bridges are plated; seen if you take off the caseback if you want to look.
35mm case, 42mm lug-to-lug, 18mm lugs. Unpolished original case. The dial and hands have some awesome patina, six decades of it. Not distressed, just aged, passed down from my grandfather, meticulously maintained.

The Omega Speedmaster Professional Apollo 11 Edition pays tribute to the first lunar landing in 1969. It preserves the heritage of the original Moonwatch while offering modern durability and precision. The stainless-steel 42 mm case features brushed and polished surfaces, a black aluminum tachymeter bezel, and a domed Hesalite crystal for an authentic look identical to the watches worn by Apollo astronauts.

The black step dial provides sharp legibility with luminous hour markers and white chronograph registers. The manual-wind Caliber 1861 movement—directly descended from the Moon-landed Caliber 321—is visible through the solid engraved caseback, inscribed “Flight-Qualified by NASA for All Manned Space Missions.”

A stainless-steel bracelet with secure folding clasp ensures balanced wear and long-term comfort. The watch includes chronograph functionality for elapsed-time measurement, tachymeter scale, and 48-hour power reserve.

This reference was produced between 2014 and 2021, marking the final generation of the pre-Master Chronometer Moonwatch before Omega introduced the Co-Axial Caliber 3861. The Apollo 11 Special Edition remains one of the most collectible and historically faithful Speedmasters, offering exceptional reliability, serviceability, and long-term value.

Omega Genève Chronostop Ref. 145.009 – Caliber 865 – Iconic 1960s Motorsport Chronograph

An excellent example of the iconic Omega Genève Chronostop reference 145.009, a minimalist chronograph produced during the golden era of Omega sports watches in the late 1960s. Designed with motorsport timing in mind, the Chronostop embodies Omega’s innovative approach to chronograph design, utilizing a single-pusher system integrated into the crown for intuitive operation.

The watch is well known for its "stop" function, allowing the seconds hand to be used as a stopwatch, returning to 12 upon a second press

This reference is powered by the Omega manual-wind Caliber 865, a robust and beautifully engineered movement derived from Omega’s respected chronograph calibers of the period. The Chronostop was marketed toward drivers and engineers who required quick, legible timing functionality without the complexity of traditional multi-register chronographs.

The stainless steel case remains sharp with honest signs of wear consistent with careful use over more than five decades. The dial retains its original character with strong legibility and attractive vintage patina, features increasingly sought after by collectors. The watch presents exactly as a well-preserved vintage Omega should.

The Chronostop models have gained increasing collector attention in recent years due to their distinctive design, historical significance, and relative rarity compared to more commonly seen Omega chronographs of the era.

The Omega Speedmaster ref. 3511.20 is part of a lesser discussed branch of the Speedmaster family, often referred to as the “Reduced” or automatic line. While the Moonwatch remained manually wound, Omega expanded the collection in the late 1980s and 1990s to include automatic chronographs that carried the same design language in a more compact and accessible format.

This reference features a white dial configuration, which offers a different look compared to the more common black dial Speedmasters. The layout remains classic with three sub registers and a tachymeter scale, but the lighter dial gives it a cleaner and more contemporary feel.

Powered by an automatic chronograph movement, the watch was designed for everyday usability while still retaining the core identity of the Speedmaster. These references have developed a following for their proportions and versatility, especially for those who prefer a slightly smaller case.

This is one of the more wearable Speedmasters. The size feels balanced, and the automatic movement makes it easy to live with day to day.

The white dial changes the character entirely. It feels lighter and more casual, while still keeping the structure and layout that make the Speedmaster recognizable.

Every watch is thoroughly vetted, carefully described with complete transparency, and guaranteed authentic. Each piece is offered in honest condition, ensuring collectors receive a timepiece that reflects both originality and integrity.

The Tudor Submariner ref. 75090 sits in an interesting place within the broader Submariner lineage. Produced in the 1990s, it carries the familiar design language established by Rolex while offering its own identity through proportions and execution. The 36mm case size is a notable departure from the standard 40mm Submariner format, originally intended for markets and wearers who preferred a more compact profile without sacrificing the functionality of a true dive watch.

This reference retains all the core elements: rotating bezel, Mercedes-style handset, and high-contrast dial layout. The blue variant, in particular, has developed a following due to how well it ages. Over time, the tritium lume softens into a creamy tone that contrasts against the blue dial and bezel, giving each example a slightly different character depending on exposure and wear.

You pick this up expecting it to feel small, but it's far from that. The 36mm case wears so well. The blue is the hook though. It’s not bright, not dull either. Just enough depth to keep catching your eye, especially next to that warm lume. It feels like a Submariner you don’t have to think about. Throw it on, go about your day, and it quietly does everything right.

The Omega Seamaster 300M Jacques Mayol is one of the more distinctive limited editions produced during the height of the Seamaster’s modern popularity in the late 1990s. Created in tribute to Jacques Mayol, the legendary free diver, this reference separates itself immediately through its green wave dial and matching bezel, a notable departure from the standard blue and black variants of the time.

Beyond the color, what makes this watch significant is how closely it ties into Omega’s real-world diving heritage. Mayol was one of the most important figures in freediving, and this collaboration reflects a more authentic connection to the ocean than many typical limited editions.

This example stands out even further as a true, complete collector’s set. It includes the rarely seen green presentation box and matching card holder, along with a signed note from Jacques Mayol himself. That level of completeness is exceptionally uncommon and elevates the watch well beyond a standard example of the reference.

Equally important is the condition. These are often found with lume that has aged or discolored, but here the dial and hands have retained their original green tone, which is becoming increasingly difficult to find.

This is one of those pieces where the details matter more than the headline. At a glance, it’s a green Seamaster. But then you notice the lume is still perfectly green, not washed out or mismatched. Then the full set. Then the signed note. It just keeps stacking.

It feels less like a watch you wear every day and more like something you pull out to appreciate for a minute. A proper collector’s piece that just happens to still be wearable.

Every watch is thoroughly vetted, carefully described with complete transparency, and guaranteed authentic. Each piece is offered in honest condition, ensuring collectors receive a timepiece that reflects both originality and integrity.

The movement in this Rare Omega f300Hz Chronometer is the Omega Caliber 1250, which is based on the ESA 9162 tuning fork movement developed by ESA (Ébauches SA).

This movement uses a tuning fork vibrating at 300 Hz to drive the hands, giving it a smooth, sweeping second hand similar to automatics, but powered by a battery.
It's *not quartz*, but electromechanical, and was very advanced for its time in the late 1960s to early 1970s.

The Omega Speedmaster Professional Moonwatch 311.30.42.30.01.005 isn’t just a watch—it’s a legend wrapped around your wrist, a mechanical masterpiece with a direct lineage to mankind’s greatest adventure. If you appreciate heritage, precision, and a timepiece that exudes pure, masculine sophistication, this is the one.

A Watch That Touched the Moon

This isn’t just another luxury watch; it’s the Moonwatch—the only timepiece officially qualified by NASA for spaceflight. It was there when Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin took their first steps on the lunar surface. This is the very same tool astronauts trusted on the Apollo missions, built for the extremes of space yet refined enough to command attention anywhere on Earth.

Timeless Design, Pure Speedmaster DNA

With its 42mm stainless steel case, black dial, and hesalite crystal, this Speedmaster is an embodiment of rugged elegance. The white hands and indexes contrast sharply against the deep black dial, ensuring perfect readability. The symmetrical case design, twisted lugs, and iconic tachymeter bezel make it instantly recognizable. Whether you wear it on a suit or with jeans, it radiates an effortless cool.

Manual-Wind Movement—A True Connection to Time

At the heart of this legend beats the Omega Caliber 1861—a manual-winding chronograph movement that’s directly descended from the one that powered Moonwatches in the 1960s. This is watchmaking at its purest—no automatic rotor, no unnecessary frills—just a beautifully engineered, hand-wound movement that keeps you connected to time itself.

The Ultimate Collector’s Set – The “Big Box” Experience

This isn’t just a watch—it’s an experience. The big box presentation set elevates this Speedmaster into collector’s territory, with accessories that make it feel like you’re unboxing history:
✅ NATO & Velcro straps – Swap out the bracelet for a rugged strap, just like the astronauts did.
✅ Loupe with Omega branding – Admire the fine details of your Moonwatch up close.
✅ Strap-changing tool – Because true enthusiasts love customization.
✅ Special Moonwatch book – A deep dive into the Speedmaster’s unparalleled space legacy.
✅ Unique presentation box – A tribute to Omega’s spacefaring history, making it a must-have for collectors.

Why You Need This Watch

Owning the Omega Speedmaster Professional isn’t about keeping time—it’s about keeping history alive. It’s about wearing a legacy, feeling the connection to space pioneers, and knowing that on your wrist sits a timepiece built for the extraordinary.

It’s not just a watch. It’s the Moonwatch. And it’s waiting

The Omega Speedmaster is a legendary timepiece that has achieved iconic status in the world of horology. Originally introduced in 1957 as a racing chronograph, the Speedmaster gained worldwide fame as the watch that accompanied NASA astronauts on all six lunar missions. In 1969, the Speedmaster became the first watch to be worn on the moon, cementing its place in history. Durability, accuracy, and reliability under extreme conditions has made it a favorite of pilots, astronauts, and adventurers alike. Over the years, Omega has continued to refine and improve the design of the Speedmaster, while staying true to its classic look and feel. Today, the Speedmaster remains one of the most popular and revered watches in the world, a true icon of Swiss watchmaking.
The Speedmaster Reduced was first revealed in 1988 and has become collectible for its smaller proportions and practicality with automatic movement. These pieces also offer great value in today's market and are versatile, being equally at home on leather or NATO-style straps. This well-preserved example, circa '97 houses an early "Swiss Made" dial and matching hands that benefit from the use of luminova lume material which is still functioning today. The dial/hands are both mint and the insert has aged gracefully with no notable wear. The case retains sharp edges and the bracelet remains very tight, fitting and approximate 7.5 inch wrist. For anyone looking for a practical and under the radar chronograph, this piece is ready for many adventures to come.

Dial: Is a luminova variant with lume that's still functional. It is mint under magnification.

Bezel insert: Fantastic preservation with no notable scuffs.

Hands: Are matching.

Case: Light signs of use consistent with age. It remains sharp with factory bevels still present. Correct pushers and signed crown.

Bracelet: Remains very tight and will fit an approximate 7.5 inch wrist. Light signs of use overall.

Crystal: Has some light wear visible in the macro photography. No cracks or chips.

Accessories: NA

The movement is running well on our timegrapher and comes with our 6-month mechanical warranty.

This 2001 Omega Speedmaster Reduced has been a deeply appreciated piece in my collection—one of those watches that quietly earns your respect the more time you spend with it.

Inspired by the legendary Moonwatch, the Reduced carries the same iconic DNA in a slightly more wearable 39mm case. It wears beautifully on the wrist—balanced, versatile, and equally at home dressed up or down. The automatic chronograph movement keeps time reliably, and the classic tri-compax dial, hesalite crystal, and tachymeter bezel give it that unmistakable Speedmaster character.

What makes this piece special to me is how effortlessly it blends history with everyday practicality. It’s a watch you can actually live with—not just admire from afar. Over the years, I’ve taken care to wear it thoughtfully and keep it in good condition, respecting both its craftsmanship and its legacy.

There are of course signs of gentle wear consistent with age, but nothing that detracts from its presence or performance—if anything, it adds to the story. This isn’t a safe queen; it’s a watch that’s been enjoyed the way it was meant to be.

I’m only letting it go to make room for another piece, but I hope it finds someone who appreciates it in the same way—someone who understands that a Speedmaster isn’t just a watch, it’s a piece of history you get to carry with you.

Happy to answer any questions or provide additional photos.

The Omega Speedmaster Professional Apollo 11 Edition pays tribute to the first lunar landing in 1969. It preserves the heritage of the original Moonwatch while offering modern durability and precision. The stainless-steel 42 mm case features brushed and polished surfaces, a black aluminum tachymeter bezel, and a domed Hesalite crystal for an authentic look identical to the watches worn by Apollo astronauts.

The black step dial provides sharp legibility with luminous hour markers and white chronograph registers. The manual-wind Caliber 1861 movement—directly descended from the Moon-landed Caliber 321—is visible through the solid engraved caseback, inscribed “Flight-Qualified by NASA for All Manned Space Missions.”

A stainless-steel bracelet with secure folding clasp ensures balanced wear and long-term comfort. The watch includes chronograph functionality for elapsed-time measurement, tachymeter scale, and 48-hour power reserve.

This reference was produced between 2014 and 2021, marking the final generation of the pre-Master Chronometer Moonwatch before Omega introduced the Co-Axial Caliber 3861. The Apollo 11 Special Edition remains one of the most collectible and historically faithful Speedmasters, offering exceptional reliability, serviceability, and long-term value.

The Omega Speedmaster Mark 4.5 (ref. 176.0012) is a vintage automatic chronograph that blends classic 1970s design with modern (for its era) mechanical reliability. It uses the robust Caliber 1045 (Lemania-5100 based) automatic movement, delivering day/date, 24-hour indication, and chronograph functionality. Though unofficially dubbed “Mark 4.5” by collectors, the 176.0012 pairs the case style of the earlier Mark IV with the automatic movement of later Speedmasters — giving it a unique place in the Speedmaster lineage.

A Classic Mid-Century Omega with Honest Patina

Offered here is a beautifully preserved vintage Omega Automatic powered by the renowned Omega Caliber 351, one of Omega’s earliest and most respected full-rotor automatic movements.

Dial & Aesthetic Condition

The dial is the true highlight of this watch. It features an original silvered finish that has developed a uniform, speckled patina over time—often referred to by collectors as a frosted or eggshell aging effect. This natural aging is consistent with mid-century Omega dials and is widely appreciated for its authenticity and character.
• Original Omega signed dial with applied gold Omega logo
• Even, attractive patina throughout—no signs of re-dialing
• Applied gold hour markers with sharp faceting
• Dauphine-style hands, period correct
• Clean outer minute track with excellent legibility

The dial aging is honest and symmetrical, enhancing the vintage appeal rather than detracting from it—exactly the type of patina seasoned collectors seek.

Case & Wear
• Gold-tone case with classic mid-century proportions
• Elegant thin bezel emphasizing dial presence
• Traditional curved lugs typical of late-1940s to early-1950s Omega design
• Paired with a green leather strap that complements the warmth of the case

Movement
• Omega Caliber 351 automatic movement
• Bumper automatic system
• 17 jewels
• Renowned for durability and smooth operation
• A cornerstone movement in Omega’s early automatic lineage

⸻

Historical Background – Omega Caliber 351

The Omega Caliber 351 was introduced in the late 1940s, during a pivotal period when Omega was establishing itself as one of the world’s premier manufacturers of reliable automatic wristwatches.

Why the Caliber 351 Matters
• It is part of Omega’s 30.10 RA PC family, derived from the legendary 30mm manual-wind calibers
• Utilizes a bumper automatic system, where the rotor oscillates between springs rather than rotating fully
• Built to military-grade standards of the era, with exceptional longevity
• Served as a direct predecessor to Omega’s later full-rotor automatic movements

These movements were used extensively during the post-WWII boom, when Omega supplied watches to professionals, engineers, and business leaders seeking precision and durability.

Collectibility

Watches powered by the Caliber 351 are increasingly collectible because:
• They represent Omega’s transition from manual wind to automatic mastery
• They were produced in smaller numbers compared to later Seamaster references
• Original dials with natural patina, like this example, are becoming scarce

⸻

Why This Watch

This Omega is not about perfection—it’s about authenticity. The dial tells a story of decades past, the movement represents a milestone in horological engineering, and the overall presence captures the elegance of post-war Swiss watchmaking.

Perfect for:
• Vintage Omega collectors
• Enthusiasts of early automatic movements
• Those who appreciate original, untouched dials with character

Since 1993, the Seamaster Diver 300M has enjoyed a legendary following. Today’s modern collection has embraced that famous ocean heritage and updated it with OMEGA’s most advanced innovation and design.

This 42 mm model is crafted in stainless steel and includes a grade 5 titanium bezel with a laser-ablated diving scale in positive relief and a single dot of white Super-LumiNova at 12 o’clock.

PVD stainless steel is used to create the brushed dial as well as the polished and skeletonized hands and indexes, which are filled with white Super-LumiNova. Other markings, including the OMEGA logo, are transferred in black.

The watch is presented on a brushed mesh bracelet with clasp, and is driven by the OMEGA Co-Axial Master Chronometer Calibre 8806, which can be seen through the sapphire-crystal caseback.

Offered for sale by NWS, of Fairfax, VA: A very good condition Omega Speedmaster Silver Snoopy Award 50th Anniversary Series watch, REF. 310.32.42.50.02.001, circa 2023.

The Omega Speedmaster Silver Snoopy Award 50th Anniversary Edition is a masterpiece that celebrates NASA’s 1970 honor to Omega for its crucial role in the Apollo 13 mission. Its stunning silver dial features an embossed Snoopy medallion, paying tribute to the beloved space icon and Omega’s deep-rooted connection to space exploration. Flip the watch over, and a mesmerizing, animated case back brings the moon journey to life, with Snoopy orbiting Earth in perfect sync with the chronograph. Powered by the Co-Axial Master Chronometer Caliber 3861, this Speedmaster delivers superior precision and magnetic resistance. The 42mm stainless steel case, paired with a striking blue nylon strap, creates a timeless yet playful aesthetic or step it up a notch and enjoy this collectors item on Speedmaster stainless steel bracelet, included as well. But no matter how you wear it, with the Silver Snoopy Award Edition, Omega blends history, innovation, and storytelling into one extraordinary timepiece you can wear on your wrist.

Omega Speedmaster Broad Arrow – Ref. 3594.50

Ready for the look of the $100k Speedmaster Calibre 321 Canopus Gold, for an under 6% of the price.

Up for sale is an Omega Speedmaster Broad Arrow, reference 3594.50, a modern tribute to the very first Speedmaster from 1957. This model blends classic vintage design cues with the reliability of a modern manual-wind chronograph.

Featuring the iconic Broad Arrow hands, a black dial with applied Omega logo, and a steel tachymeter bezel, this reference stands out as one of the most distinctive Speedmasters produced in the 2000s.

The watch is powered by the Omega Caliber 1861, a proven hand-wound movement descended from the legendary Moonwatch lineage. The 42mm stainless steel case wears beautifully on the wrist and offers timeless proportions that work for both daily wear and collection rotation.

The Bracelet I paired with this watch is closer to what it would have come with, back when it was new. It also gives it that Calibre 321 Canopus Gold look, a watch that lists for $100k! The manufacturer of the bracelet is Forstner JB Champion, the company who made bracelets for Omega back in the day.

The watch is in great condition. No deep scratches or dings, just so light microscopic swirl marks. The hesalite crystal does have light swirls, but polywatch would do the trick (and I’ve ever heard Tim Mosso say a pencil’s rubber eraser), but it’s hardly noticeable. One of the two OEM straps is in used condition, and was dyed a darker brown color. The second one, that on the deployment buckle, is in new condition. The bracelet, that the watch is on, is in lightly used condition and comes with extra links. The watch will also come with an Omega OEM red travel case.

Details:
• Reference: 3594.50
• Case size: 42mm
• Stainless steel case
• Black dial with applied logo
• Broad Arrow hands
• Steel tachymeter bezel
• Manual-wind Omega Cal. 1861
• Hesalite crystal
• 2 Original Omega straps with 1 deployment clasp
• White cardboard outer box & red watch box
• Warranty card, card holder, booklet, & travel case

The watch has had no issues during my ownership. It is being sold as described, as shown, and as is, with the understanding that routine service or future maintenance is the responsibility of the buyer.

This Broad Arrow has become increasingly sought after due to its vintage styling, short production run, and connection to Speedmaster history. A fantastic option for collectors looking for something a bit different from the standard Moonwatch.

The Omega Speedmaster Professional Apollo 11 Edition pays tribute to the first lunar landing in 1969. It preserves the heritage of the original Moonwatch while offering modern durability and precision. The stainless-steel 42 mm case features brushed and polished surfaces, a black aluminum tachymeter bezel, and a domed Hesalite crystal for an authentic look identical to the watches worn by Apollo astronauts.

The black step dial provides sharp legibility with luminous hour markers and white chronograph registers. The manual-wind Caliber 1861 movement—directly descended from the Moon-landed Caliber 321—is visible through the solid engraved caseback, inscribed “Flight-Qualified by NASA for All Manned Space Missions.”

A stainless-steel bracelet with secure folding clasp ensures balanced wear and long-term comfort. The watch includes chronograph functionality for elapsed-time measurement, tachymeter scale, and 48-hour power reserve.

This reference was produced between 2014 and 2021, marking the final generation of the pre-Master Chronometer Moonwatch before Omega introduced the Co-Axial Caliber 3861. The Apollo 11 Special Edition remains one of the most collectible and historically faithful Speedmasters, offering exceptional reliability, serviceability, and long-term value.

Vorwort:
Die Omega Seamaster Ref. 196.0079 verkörpert den typischen Charme klassischer Omega Vintage-Modelle – elegant, stilvoll und mit einer Ausstrahlung, die sofort begeistert.
Besonders hervorzuheben ist das außergewöhnliche gebürstete Zifferblatt, das je nach Lichteinfall wunderschön schimmert und der Uhr eine sehr edle, fast schon textile Optik verleiht. Genau diese feinen Details machen Vintage-Omegas so besonders und verleihen ihnen eine ganz eigene Persönlichkeit.
Die klare Gestaltung sorgt für eine zeitlose Eleganz, die sich sowohl im Alltag als auch zu formellen Anlässen perfekt tragen lässt. Eine Uhr, die dezent wirkt und gerade dadurch ihre Klasse zeigt.
Ihr Exemplar befindet sich in einem sehr schönen Zustand und überzeugt durch seinen authentischen Vintage-Charakter.

Omega Seamaster Diver 300M (Ref. 212.30.41.20.03.001) – Klassischer Taucher mit maritimer DNA
Gehäuse und Design

Die Omega Seamaster Diver 300M Ref. 212.30.41.20.03.001 ist eine der bekanntesten Taucheruhren der Marke und verbindet sportliche Funktionalität mit zeitloser Eleganz. Das 41 mm große Edelstahlgehäuse überzeugt durch seine ausgewogenen Proportionen und die Kombination aus satinierten und polierten Oberflächen.

Die einseitig drehbare Taucherlünette mit blauer Aluminium-Einlage verleiht der Uhr ihren maritimen Charakter und ermöglicht eine präzise Zeitmessung unter Wasser. Das blaue Zifferblatt mit dem typischen Wellenmuster ist ein ikonisches Merkmal der Seamaster-Reihe und sorgt für einen hohen Wiedererkennungswert.

Skelettierte Zeiger und applizierte Indizes mit Leuchtmasse garantieren beste Ablesbarkeit bei allen Lichtverhältnissen. Geschützt wird das Zifferblatt durch ein kratzfestes, beidseitig entspiegeltes Saphirglas.

Uhrwerk und Technik

Im Inneren arbeitet das bewährte Omega Co-Axial Kaliber 2500, ein automatisches Uhrwerk mit Co-Axial-Hemmung, das für hohe Präzision und reduzierte Reibung steht.

Das Werk bietet eine Gangreserve von ca. 48 Stunden und überzeugt durch zuverlässige Leistung im Alltag. Neben der klassischen Stunden-, Minuten- und Sekundenanzeige verfügt die Uhr über eine Datumsanzeige bei 3 Uhr, die sich bequem einstellen lässt.

Armband und Tragekomfort

Die Uhr wird am charakteristischen Edelstahlarmband von Omega getragen, das durch seine geschwungene Form und die Kombination aus polierten und satinierten Elementen sofort erkennbar ist.

Die robuste Faltschließe sorgt für sicheren Halt, während die ergonomische Konstruktion des Bandes einen hohen Tragekomfort gewährleistet. Die Uhr sitzt ausgewogen am Handgelenk und eignet sich sowohl für sportliche Aktivitäten als auch für den Alltag.

Funktionalität

Die Seamaster Diver 300M ist als professionelle Taucheruhr konzipiert und bietet eine Vielzahl funktionaler Eigenschaften:

Wasserdichtigkeit bis 300 Meter (30 bar)
Heliumauslassventil bei 10 Uhr
Einseitig drehbare Taucherlünette
Leuchtzeiger und -indizes für optimale Ablesbarkeit
Verschraubte Krone für zusätzliche Sicherheit

Diese Eigenschaften machen sie zu einem zuverlässigen Begleiter sowohl unter Wasser als auch an Land.

Fazit

Die Omega Seamaster Diver 300M Ref. 212.30.41.20.03.001 ist ein echter Klassiker unter den Taucheruhren. Mit ihrem markanten Wellenzifferblatt, dem zuverlässigen Co-Axial-Werk und der hohen Alltagstauglichkeit vereint sie sportliche Performance mit zeitloser Eleganz.

The Speedmaster Moonwatch is one of the world’s most iconic timepieces. Having been a part of all six moon landings, the legendary chronograph is an impressive representation of the brand’s adventurous pioneering spirit.

All original with a replacement aftermarket leather band. This watch was procured from an estate as part of a set of mans and woman’s Omega Deville. As far as I know this watch has never been opened or serviced. The watch runs flawlessly, keeping great time, so I never opened the watch. The watch was tested with a Kee Gold & Platinum Tester (see pic) verifying it was 18K Gold. (“The 1967 Omega De Ville reference "tool 106" is typically a monocoque (one-piece) case, which means it does not have a traditional removable case back. Instead, the movement is removed from the front of the watch.
Procedure for a Monocoque Case:
Opening a monocoque case requires specialized tools and a delicate touch to avoid damage. It is often best left to a professional watchmaker.”) This is why it was electronically tested with the Kee Gold Tester to be 18K solid yellow gold.

“THE DE VILLE IS BORN
In 1967 the De Ville dropped "Seamaster" from its name and became a line in its own right. At its launch, the De Ville generated considerable attention due to the fact that it combined OMEGA's already-proven movements with meticulous finish and design.
Simpler, slimmer and more varied, the collection was instantly appreciated for its refined form.
Since then, the timeless good looks of the De Ville have remained untouched. Inside the case, the timepieces in the De Ville family have become equally as impressive.
Today, they are also equipped with OMEGA's state-of-the-industry technology.” Omega.com

“Omega Seamaster DeVille - Henry Golding - Crazy Rich Asians

1960's Omega Seamaster Deville with 18ct gold case (the watch in the film has a Date window at the 3 o'clock position)

Henry Golding wears a vintage Omega Seamaster Deville gold watch with black strap in Crazy Rich Asians (2018).

Henry Golding's own watch that he wore in the film, as told in a GQ interview.


The watch has a bold case and black leather strap. It looks like a vintage Omega Seamaster DeVille, but this needs to be confirmed with a better photo. if you know the brand and model of the watch, please contact Watch-ID here.

UPDATE April 2021: Thanks to a message via the contact form from Mingu Jeong, we now can confirm that this is indeed a vintage Omega Seamaster DeVille. An interview with GQ on October 2, 2018, with Henry Golding verifies that he was indeed wearing an Omega Seamaster De Ville (vintage). See YouTube video (timestamp 0:54).

The vintage Omega is part of Henry Golding's private collection, and he wore his own watch in the film. We can see in the close-up of the watch in the GQ interview that Golding's watch has a date window at the 3 o'clock position.”

COMMENT
This classic chronograph features a matte navy dial with sub-registers positioned at 3, 6, and 9 o’clock for the 30-minute counter, 12-hour counter, and small seconds, respectively. A finely detailed tachymeter scale encircles the outer edge, emphasizing its functional precision. The hour, minute, and central seconds hands are accented in orange for enhanced legibility and dynamic contrast, while the central chronograph and sub-register hands are rendered in white, lending a balanced and sporty aesthetic. Both the hands and indexes are filled with luminous material to ensure readability in low light. The case, with its C-line silhouette, reflects the bold, ergonomic design language of the 1970s, and retains its original brushed finish on the upper surface.
The case remains in excellent condition, with only minimal surface marks and a clearly preserved sunray texture. The lugs and flanks show no dents, maintaining the crisp integrity of the case form. The dial has not aged, retaining its matte texture and rich coloration. The hands and indexes show no fading or tarnish, and the luminous material has aged evenly. Fully serviced by our watchmakers.
The C-line—or cushion-shaped—case design stands as one of the defining aesthetics of 1970s chronographs. Released as part of the Seamaster lineage during the same era that saw the rise of the Speedmaster Mark II, this model reflects Omega’s dedication to precision and durability. OVERVIEW

This is an authentic Omega Speedmaster Professional Moonwatch ref. 145.022, powered by the legendary Calibre 861 manual-winding movement. Measuring 42MM, this reference represents one of the most important eras of the Speedmaster line and remains widely appreciated by collectors for its classic proportions and enduring vintage character.

This example was sold in 1975 and presents in good vintage condition, with wear throughout consistent with age.

The movement carries a 31 million serial, dating production to approximately 1969, while the caseback is stamped 1971. It was not uncommon for Omega during this period to produce movements first, case them later, and ultimately sell the watch several years after initial movement production. Such production and sales timelines are well documented within vintage Speedmaster scholarship and are consistent with Omega manufacturing practices of the era.

The chronograph functions operate properly, and the watch is keeping excellent time.

The original paperwork shows two different pen colors, which is typical for handwritten documents from the 1970s. It was common for different individuals, such as a salesperson and manager, to complete separate sections of the paperwork.

The watch comes fitted on a brown factory Omega NATO strap and includes box and papers. It is backed by a 1-year warranty for US customers for added peace of mind.

The watch was tested with a Kee Gold & Platinum Tester (see pic) verifying it was 18K Gold. (“The 1967 Omega De Ville reference "tool 106" is typically a monocoque (one-piece) case, which means it does not have a traditional removable case back. Instead, the movement is removed from the front of the watch.
Procedure for a Monocoque Case:
Opening a monocoque case requires specialized tools and a delicate touch to avoid damage. It is often best left to a professional watchmaker.”) This is why it was electronically tested with the Kee Gold Tester to be 18K solid yellow gold.

“THE DE VILLE IS BORN
In 1967 the De Ville dropped "Seamaster" from its name and became a line in its own right. At its launch, the De Ville generated considerable attention due to the fact that it combined OMEGA's already-proven movements with meticulous finish and design.
Simpler, slimmer and more varied, the collection was instantly appreciated for its refined form.
Since then, the timeless good looks of the De Ville have remained untouched. Inside the case, the timepieces in the De Ville family have become equally as impressive.
Today, they are also equipped with OMEGA's state-of-the-industry technology.” Omega.com

NAMED ONE OF WATCHPRO MAGAZINE’S “HOT 100” - THE TOP 100 INFLUENCERS OF THE U.S. WATCH INDUSTRY. HOW CAN WE SERVE YOU?

1966 Omega (Seamaster) Rare Cal. 560 Vintage Mens 10K Gold Filled & Stainless Steel Watch - Only Approx. 3000 Made

Rare Movement: Only 3,000 Made - Pleasing Original Dial - 1 Year Warranty

Today's offering showcases this rare, classic and pleasing automatic Omega. It features Omega's rare caliber 560 movement of which only 3,000 examples were made. These watches were made just for the U.S. market to avoid higher tariffs, and these 3,000 movements were split across 9 models, making each model in effect a very "limited edition."

It has fully Omega parts and presents beautifully on the wrist. Its untouched original silver dial is in excellent condition with light aging, and its gold-filled top "tropical" steel back case has the look of a solid gold Omega without the price tag.

In construction it is a Seamaster, but this model was only very rarely branded so on the dial.

The case shows a slivers of gold wearthrough on the high points of the lugs as is common with these - and the back has marks from previous opening. It has not been previously heavily polished so retains strong lines and presents nicely on the wrist.

One of our Master Watchmakers has fully serviced the watch which consists of fully disassembling the movement, carefully inspecting each part, installing a correct movement clasp and new armored crystal, reassembling and lubricating the movement, and extensive timing testing and regulation (a $695 value).

We have also timed it on a machine that simulates it being worn on the wrist. It is keeping very accurate time.

Omega is undeniably one of the most historically important timepiece manufactures of all time. In fact, the Speedmaster is probably the most important wrist watch of all time.

As a result, the Speedmaster casts a massive shadow over all of its siblings.... even over firmly planted models like the Seamaster and Constellation ranges.

The resulting effect is double sided: many vintage Omegas, we feel are incredibly undervalued in the market as it exists today, with the other side of that being that hidden gems are shocking attainable.

Take the piece that we haver here, a stainless steel Calatrava-style time-only watch dating to circa 1944.

This Ref. 2383-6 has a ton going for it. The 34.5mm case remains unpolished, with crisp lug holes and symmetrical proportions. It also features a large crown at 3 o'clock, a screw-down caseback, and Omega's own Cal. 30T2 manual-winding movement inside. The dial however, is the highlight of this particular example. The silver portion of the 'sector' design has developed a gorgeous and even 'antique' patina from age, with deeply patinated 'Arabic' indices, a 'concentric' subsidiary register at the 6 o'clock position, and a matching 'syringe' handset.

This piece, with its sizing, condition, and completely unique dial really embodies everything that makes vintage great. What more can you ask for?

Condition:

Case: Unpolished, with symmetrical lugs at all 4 corners and even lug holes. Shows normal signs of wear throughout with no significant blemishes or damage.

Dial: Exhibits a stunning, gentle patina across the surface, showing only faint wear within the subsidiary register and text. The Radium ‘Arabic’ indices remain fully-intact and exhibit a rich and uniform patina. Correct ‘syringe’ handset matches perfectly to the naked eye and under UV inspection.

Movement:

Manual-winding Calibre 30T2 by Omega.

Bracelet/Strap:

Pictured Molequin X Oliver & Clarke 'Alzan' leather strap with steel buckle.

About


The Omega Seamaster Diver Stainless Steel with a Black Dial is an iconic luxury dive watch renowned for precision and durability. Its 41mm stainless steel case combines strength with refined elegance. The unidirectional rotating bezel features a black ceramic insert, offering precise dive timing and a bold sporty look.

The black dial provides excellent contrast with luminous hands and markers, ensuring superior legibility underwater or in low light. A date window at six o’clock adds practical functionality. The watch is water-resistant up to 300 meters, making it ideal for professional divers and enthusiasts alike.

This model features Omega’s Co-Axial Caliber 2500, delivering exceptional accuracy and long-term reliability. Its movement reduces friction, extends service intervals, and ensures smooth operation. The sapphire crystal protects the dial while offering scratch resistance and clarity.

The stainless steel bracelet combines robustness with comfort, making it perfect for daily wear. Its historical significance ties to Omega’s long-standing maritime heritage and role as the official timekeeper of numerous diving expeditions.

The Omega Seamaster Diver 212.30.41.20.01.003 blends technical mastery with sophisticated style. Its enduring design, professional functionality, and collectible value make it a must-have for watch enthusiasts and collectors.

The Omega Geneve line was introduced in the 1950s to commemorate Omega's success in the Geneva Observatory trials, which its models dominated with high precision. Initially, it included Omega's best hand-wound and some chronometer models, but the line evolved over the decades to feature automatic movements, more diverse designs like the "Dynamic" models in the late 1960s, and a wide range of variations until it was eventually discontinued.

The Omega Speedmaster Date Ref. 3511.70 is a distinctive and collectible variation within the Speedmaster lineup, produced in the late 1990s and known for its rich green dial and sporty chronograph design. This reference stands out from traditional Speedmaster models with its unique colorway and compact proportions, offering a refreshing and uncommon take on Omega’s iconic chronograph.

The deep green dial, often nicknamed the “Golf Dial,” features a classic tri-compax layout with contrasting subdials, giving the watch a subtle reverse panda aesthetic. Luminous hands and hour markers provide strong legibility, while the stainless steel tachymeter bezel reinforces the Speedmaster’s racing heritage. Its versatile 39mm case makes it extremely wearable while still delivering strong wrist presence.

Powered by the Omega automatic Calibre 1155, based on the proven Valjoux 7750 architecture, the watch offers reliable and robust chronograph performance along with a practical date function. This movement is known for its durability and ease of service, making it a solid everyday chronograph option.

This particular example is accompanied by its original card dated 1999, adding an extra layer of collectibility and provenance to an already uncommon Speedmaster reference.

This 50th anniversary timepiece pays tribute to the “Silver Snoopy Award” that OMEGA received from NASA astronauts in 1970, in recognition of the brand’s contributions to space exploration, including the Apollo 13 mission.

The 42 mm case in stainless steel is presented on a blue nylon fabric strap, which features the trajectory of the Apollo 13 mission on the embossed lining. The silver dial has blue PVD angle-shaped hour markers and hands, as well as an embossed silver Snoopy medallion on the blue subdial at 9 o’clock.

On the NAIAD LOCK caseback, a vision of the far side of the moon has been decorated on the sapphire crystal using micro-structured metallisation. An animated Snoopy in his Command and Service Module (CSM) is shown on a magical hand, which rotates when the chronograph function is used. Behind, an Earth disc rotates once per minute in sync with the watch’s small seconds hand.

Along with black engravings on the caseback in tribute to Apollo 13 and OMEGA's Silver Snoopy Award, there is also a blue ceramic [ZrO2] bezel ring with a white enamel tachymeter scale. The non-limited watch is driven by the OMEGA Co-Axial Master Chronometer Calibre 3861

The just released second generation of the Omega Speedmaster Moonwatch Apollo 8 Dark Side of the Moon reference 310.92.44.50.01.001.

In this 44.25 mm model, the Speedmaster’s history aboard the Apollo 8 mission is celebrated through the blackened movement, which is laser-ablated on the main plate and bridges to represent the moon’s surface. The near side can be seen through the skeletonized black anodised aluminium dial, while the far side can be seen through the caseback.

Presented on a perforated black rubber strap with yellow rubber interior, the entire watch is black ceramic and includes a varnished yellow chronograph seconds hands and a laser-engraved tachymeter scale on the bezel which is filled with white enamel. The small-seconds subdial (patent pending) also includes a grade 5 titanium hand shaped like the Saturn V rocket. This 3D shape is obtained by laser turning, while the colours are obtained by white varnish, ablation, and laser blackening.

The specially decorated movement is the OMEGA Co-Axial Master Chronometer Calibre 3869, while the engravings on the caseback include “APOLLO 8, DEC 1968”, “DARK SIDE OF THE MOON”, “WE’LL SEE YOU ON THE OTHER SIDE”, and "CO-AXIAL MASTER CHRONOMETER".

This watch is new unworn direct from an Omega authorized dealer.

Designed in collaboration with the Mercedes-AMG F1 team, this reference reflects IWC’s long-standing partnership with elite motorsport engineering.

Crafted from lightweight titanium, the 41mm case offers exceptional durability and comfort while maintaining a purposeful tool-watch aesthetic. The matte black dial is accented with striking Petronas turquoise markers and chronograph hands, delivering bold contrast and excellent legibility—hallmarks of IWC pilot watches.

The chronograph layout is clean and functional, with day-date display at 3 o’clock and large luminous Arabic numerals for optimal readability in all conditions. The turquoise rubber strap enhances the sporty character of the watch and reinforces its direct connection to the Mercedes-AMG Petronas Formula One team.

Powering the watch is IWC’s automatic chronograph movement, offering reliable performance and precision for daily wear. With its racing pedigree, lightweight construction, and distinctive color accents, this reference stands out as one of the most recognizable modern IWC pilot chronographs.

This 2023 example is offered in great pre-owned condition and comes as a complete set, making it an excellent choice for collectors, motorsport enthusiasts, or anyone seeking a contemporary IWC with strong design identity.

Inspired by the original wave-dial introduced in 1994, this diver brings that design back with a modern twist. Featuring a rich blue ceramic dial with precise laser-engraving, it is lightweight on the wrist and built for active days - making it a great companion for warm-weather wear or casual adventures near the water.

Presents itself in mint condition with little to no signs of wear. The blue dial features the signature wave pattern, which also appears on the ceramic bezel, exhibition caseback, and even underneath the blue rubber strap. The stainless steel case combines brushed and high-polished finishes for added depth, while the unidirectional ceramic bezel is fitted with a white enamel diving scale designed for easy readability underwater.

Functions include hours, minutes, seconds, date, unidirectional bezel, and helium escape valve. For something that can withstand 300 meters underwater, the watch is equipped with a helium escape valve at the 10 o’clock position - a key feature for professional divers. It allows helium to safely vent during decompression, preventing internal pressure buildup that could damage the case after extended deep-sea exposure. The unidirectional bezel rotates only counterclockwise, ensuring that any accidental movement can only show more time elapsed, never less - a critical safety measure underwater. It also features luminous hour markers and hands to further enhance legibility in low-light or underwater conditions.

Comes as a full set, boxed with warranty documentation.

If you're after a diver that honors its past while still fitting into today’s everyday wear, this one delivers. The blue wave dial brings back the 1994 design with sharper detail, while the lightweight case makes it ideal for collectors and pros who want something sporty and easy to wear.

Offered here is a 1999 Omega Seamaster Professional 300M Quartz, one of the most recognizable and wearable dive watches of the late 1990s. This reference represents the exact era that cemented the Seamaster as a modern classic—sleek, purposeful, and unmistakably Omega.

The blue wave dial and matching blue bezel are signature design elements of this generation, paired with bold luminous markers and skeletonized hands for excellent legibility. At 36mm, the case wears extremely well on the wrist—sporty without feeling oversized—and remains a sweet spot for everyday use.

Powered by Omega’s high-accuracy quartz movement, this Seamaster delivers true grab-and-go convenience while maintaining the robustness expected from a professional dive watch. With a 300m / 1000ft water-resistance rating, screw-down crown, and solid steel construction, it’s built for real-world wear, not just the display case.

The watch is fitted with the original Omega stainless steel bracelet featuring the double safety deployant clasp, giving it a secure and comfortable fit. Condition is described as good with visible signs of wear, consistent with careful use over time and appropriate for a watch of this age. The dial has not been repainted, preserving originality.

Original tritium lume, now aged to a warm cream tone, consistent with late-1990s production. Lume is no longer reactive like modern Super-LumiNova, as expected for tritium, and contributes to the watch’s authentic Bond-era character.

This example has been serviced/revised, offering peace of mind for its next owner.

The exterior section of the dial is sun-brushed blue and includes vertical teak stripes and a circle of global destinations printed in red (GMT), silvery (+1h in summer) and blue (places without daylight savings). The centre of the dial contains a 24-hour glass ring, with light blue to indicate daytime and dark blue to indicate night. The vision of Earth is created on a grade 5 titanium plate. Omega Genève Chronostop Ref. 145.009 – Caliber 865 – Iconic 1960s Motorsport Chronograph

An excellent example of the iconic Omega Genève Chronostop reference 145.009, a minimalist chronograph produced during the golden era of Omega sports watches in the late 1960s. Designed with motorsport timing in mind, the Chronostop embodies Omega’s innovative approach to chronograph design, utilizing a single-pusher system integrated into the crown for intuitive operation.

The watch is well known for its "stop" function, allowing the seconds hand to be used as a stopwatch, returning to 12 upon a second press

This reference is powered by the Omega manual-wind Caliber 865, a robust and beautifully engineered movement derived from Omega’s respected chronograph calibers of the period. The Chronostop was marketed toward drivers and engineers who required quick, legible timing functionality without the complexity of traditional multi-register chronographs.

The stainless steel case remains sharp with honest signs of wear consistent with careful use over more than five decades. The dial retains its original character with strong legibility and attractive vintage patina, features increasingly sought after by collectors. The watch presents exactly as a well-preserved vintage Omega should.

The Chronostop models have gained increasing collector attention in recent years due to their distinctive design, historical significance, and relative rarity compared to more commonly seen Omega chronographs of the era.

The Speedmaster Moonwatch ref. 310.60.42.50.10.001 represents the modern evolution of Omega’s most historically important chronograph, executed here in solid 18k yellow gold with a green dial and matching green ceramic bezel. While the Speedmaster’s legacy is rooted in its role as NASA’s flight qualified chronograph, this reference shifts the focus toward Omega’s contemporary technical capabilities without abandoning the core Moonwatch design language.

Introduced as part of the current Moonwatch generation, this reference features Omega’s Master Chronometer certified caliber 3861, bringing co-axial escapement technology, METAS certification, and enhanced magnetic resistance into the Moonwatch platform. Visually, the watch stays true to the classic stepped dial layout and asymmetric case profile, while the green dial and green ceramic tachymeter bezel give it a distinctly modern identity.

Gold Speedmasters have long existed alongside the steel Moonwatch, but this reference stands apart due to its updated movement architecture and contemporary finishing standards. It represents Omega’s effort to merge heritage, precious metal execution, and modern chronometry into a single, cohesive package.

This is a Speedmaster that feels substantial and intentional. The weight of the solid gold case and bracelet is immediately noticeable, but the familiar Moonwatch proportions keep it wearable. The green dial and bezel combination adds depth and contrast without overpowering the design, making this a compelling option for someone who appreciates Speedmaster history but wants something far removed from the standard steel configuration.

Every watch is carefully inspected, fully vetted, and described with full transparency. This Omega is guaranteed authentic and comes backed by our commitment to honesty, quality, and trust.

This newly released 42mm Omega Seamaster Diver 300m is a perfect blend of classic aesthetics and modern innovation. Crafted in stainless steel, the watch features a black oxalic anodized aluminum bezel ring with a diving scale coated in white Super-LumiNova for superior visibility in low-light conditions.

The black aluminum dial is laser-engraved with the Seamaster’s signature wave pattern, adding depth and texture to its refined design. Skeletonized rhodium-plated hands and indexes are filled with white Super-LumiNova for optimal readability, while the Omega logo and additional markings are transferred in crisp white for a striking contrast.

Designed for versatility and durability, the watch is fitted with a brushed stainless steel mesh bracelet, offering both comfort and a sleek, modern look. Powering this timepiece is the Omega Co-Axial Master Chronometer Calibre 8806, a highly precise and reliable movement that meets the highest standards of accuracy and magnetic resistance. Visible through the sapphire-crystal caseback, this movement is a testament to Omega’s commitment to exceptional craftsmanship.

Omega Seamaster GMT Ref. 2534.50.00 is a versatile travel watch from the iconic Seamaster Professional collection, combining Omega’s renowned dive watch design with the added functionality of a GMT complication. The watch features a 41mm stainless steel case paired with a black wave-pattern dial and a matching 24-hour bi-directional rotating bezel. The dial is highly legible with luminous hands and hour markers, while the distinctive red GMT hand allows the wearer to track a second time zone, making it a practical companion for frequent travelers.

Powered by the Omega automatic Calibre 1128 movement, the Seamaster GMT offers reliable performance and smooth operation. Based on the proven Calibre 1120, the 1128 adds a true GMT function while maintaining chronometer-level accuracy and durability. Unlike the standard Diver 300M models, this GMT variant does not feature a helium escape valve, resulting in a cleaner case profile. With 300 meters of water resistance and a robust stainless steel bracelet, this Seamaster delivers both professional capability and everyday versatility in a timeless and sporty design.

Omega is undeniably one of the most historically important timepiece manufactures of all time. In fact, the Speedmaster is probably the most important wrist watch of all time.

As a result, the Speedmaster casts a massive shadow over all of its siblings.... even over firmly planted models like the Seamaster and Constellation ranges.

The resulting effect is double sided: many vintage Omegas, we feel are incredibly undervalued in the market as it exists today, with the other side of that being that hidden gems are shocking attainable.

Take the piece that we haver here, a stainless steel Calatrava-style time-only watch dating to circa 1944.

This Ref. 2383-6 has a ton going for it. The 34.5mm case remains unpolished, with crisp lug holes and symmetrical proportions. It also features a large crown at 3 o'clock, a screw-down caseback, and Omega's own Cal. 30T2 manual-winding movement inside. The dial however, is the highlight of this particular example. The silver portion of the 'sector' design has developed a gorgeous and even 'antique' patina from age, with deeply patinated 'Arabic' indices, a 'concentric' subsidiary register at the 6 o'clock position, and a matching 'syringe' handset.

This piece, with its sizing, condition, and completely unique dial really embodies everything that makes vintage great. What more can you ask for?

Condition:

Case: Unpolished, with symmetrical lugs at all 4 corners and even lug holes. Shows normal signs of wear throughout with no significant blemishes or damage.

Dial: Exhibits a stunning, gentle patina across the surface, showing only faint wear within the subsidiary register and text. The Radium ‘Arabic’ indices remain fully-intact and exhibit a rich and uniform patina. Correct ‘syringe’ handset matches perfectly to the naked eye and under UV inspection.

Movement:

Manual-winding Calibre 30T2 by Omega.

Bracelet/Strap:

Pictured Molequin X Oliver & Clarke 'Alzan' leather strap with steel buckle.

The Omega Geneve line was introduced in the 1950s to commemorate Omega's success in the Geneva Observatory trials, which its models dominated with high precision. Initially, it included Omega's best hand-wound and some chronometer models, but the line evolved over the decades to feature automatic movements, more diverse designs like the "Dynamic" models in the late 1960s, and a wide range of variations until it was eventually discontinued.

A stunning and timeless Omega Genève 166.099 from 1972. This watch epitomizes the elegance and precision of Swiss watchmaking in the 1970s, with a bold and distinctive design characterized by sharp, decisive lines, gentian in shape, typical of the period. It has a diameter of 35mm x 40 mm.
